issues.title: ElasticSearch doSimilaritySearch broken after recent changes in Document
issues.body:
After update to the most recent version the similarity search gives an exception because the Document is no longer compatible to the data return by the search hits
1. Document (org.springframework.ai.document.Document) is directly serialized from the ES response using standard jackson map to object serialization

```
doSimilaritySearch(SearchRequest searchRequest)
...
			SearchResponse<Document> res = this.elasticsearchClient.search(
					sr -> sr.index(this.options.getIndexName())
						.knn(knn -> knn.queryVector(EmbeddingUtils.toList(vectors))
							.similarity(finalThreshold)
							.k((long) searchRequest.getTopK())
							.field("embedding")
							.numCandidates((long) (1.5 * searchRequest.getTopK()))
							.filter(fl -> fl.queryString(
									qs -> qs.query(getElasticsearchQueryString(searchRequest.getFilterExpression()))))),
			---->		Document.class);

			return res.hits().hits().stream().map(this::toDocument).collect(Collectors.toList());
```
2. The previous Document class was compatible with ES responses
```
"_source":{
"embedding":[…],
"content": "...",
"media":[],
"metadata":{ .... }
```

Now, it isn´t anymore in the Document class, after [#1794](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-ai/pull/1794/commits/d262c4cd52db08f4d03e1b26eb6fb60039c1283b).
context has been renamed to text, media is no longer an array

3. Therefore I get the error
```
com.fasterxml.jackson.databind.exc.MismatchedInputException: Cannot deserialize value of type `org.springframework.ai.model.Media` from Array value (token `JsonToken.START_ARRAY`)
 at [Source: REDACTED (`StreamReadFeature.INCLUDE_SOURCE_IN_LOCATION` disabled); line: 1, column: 8428] (through reference chain: org.springframework.ai.document.Document["media"])
```
I'm not able to use ElasticSearch queries through the Vector Store.

comments.body:
@ThomasVitale , your change in Document **broke** the ElasticSearch doSimilaritySearch method because the Document class was used directly for deserializing responses and it is not compatible anymore (see above). I think a compatible ESDocument should be used and then mapped to the new one in the toDocument method.
Why didn't anyone address this issue ? It´s not causing problems to other users of ElasticSearch queries ?

@jcgouveia thanks for reporting this issue. The change of mine you referenced (#1794) introduces a new `score` field in the `Document` class, which maintains the backward compatibility for the Elasticsearch vector store integration.

The Jackson exception you got seems to be due to a different change that happened in the `Document` class: the type of the `media` field was changed from a `Collection<Media>` to a `Media` (in #1883). During deserialisation, Jackson fails turning a `media` JSON array into a something that is not an array/collection.

You're right @ThomasVitale , sorry for the mistake. Indeed the breaking change was introduced by @markpollack change you refer. But this is preventing me to move on to M5 or the SNAPSHOT version. How can I proceed? Should I solve the situation and ask for a pull request, or should it be done by the original developpers ?
@markpollack , any ideia when this could be solved ? I cannot use ElasticSearch queries with the current version.
I'm having the same problem. Still no solution?

@jcgouveia 
I modified it so that custom embedding is possible while still using record.
Does this approach align with what you had in mind?

https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-ai/pull/2651

So just to confirm, is the issue that data saved using an old version can't be loaded with the new version?

Also, does the same problem occur even when data is saved and loaded using only the new version?

Both cases, because it is saved with "content" but is read to  "text" if serialised into the new version of the spring Document, since the breaking change is in that field.
To make sure it works the Document type should be the same (the record ElasticSearchDocument)

I was indeed testing on version M6, where the bug exists. 
In the develop/snapshot with the definition you pointed on Document in fact now it works because context => text conversion is ocurring. 👍 

But data miration is something that will be necessary, for those we have ES indexes created with previous versions, because  in that case the exception is relatede with the Media field (was stored as an array, now its a single field)
I will have to create some procedures for that migration in my environment.
Since the issue is resolved, we are closing it. Please feel free to reopen if needed. Thanks!
